articles.title: Chess Invariants
articles.summary: Chess invariants are crucial for modeling and reasoning about concurrent systems like chess. Invariants help ensure that the system remains consistent and predictable by defining what must always be true. State invariants, such as TypeOK and BothKingsOnBoard, provide basic checks on a system's state, while transition invariants, including TurnParity and NotBothInCheck, describe how changes to the system affect its state. These invariants are particularly useful for analyzing chess-like systems where interleaved execution is common, making them a key technology for developers working with concurrent systems.

articles.title: A case against Boolean logic
articles.summary: Aβu∑e ∅f ℕotλtion --> writings on math, logic, philosophy and art The case against boolean logic In our ambition to discover ideas that are all-encompassing and eternal, we often find ourselves unable to think clearly even about simple issues with obvious solutions. Boolean thinking is a pattern of thought that assumes every statement should necessarily be categorized as either true or false, but this principle can lead to contradictions when context is taken into account. By assuming that statements are always true or false regardless of the context, we may overlook imperfections and flaws in our thinking agents.

articles.title: Megalodon: Mass GitHub Repo Backdooring via CI Workflows
articles.summary: An automated campaign using GitHub Actions workflows pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories in a six-hour window. The attacks injected base64-encoded bash payloads that exfiltrated CI secrets, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and source code secrets to a C2 server. The campaigns deployed two payload variants: the mass variant SysDiag adds new workflows triggered on every push and pull request, while the targeted variant Optimize-Build replaces existing workflows with workflow_dispatch triggers.

articles.title: Slumber a TUI HTTP Client
articles.summary: Slumber is a terminal-based HTTP client that allows developers to interact with REST and other HTTP clients without needing to write code. It supports both Terminal User Interface (TUI) and Command Line Interface (CLI) modes, making it easy to use and configure. The TUI mode provides an interactive experience for sending requests and viewing responses, while the CLI mode is useful for sending quick requests and scripting. Slumber's goal is to be easy to use, configurable, and sharable. To that end, configuration is defined in a YAML file called the request collection, which is shared between both modes of operation.

articles.title: Gnutella: A Protocol Outliving the World That Created It
articles.summary: Gnutella is a decentralized file-sharing protocol that emerged in the early 2000s and has since become one of the most popular online services. Despite its success, Gnutella faced challenges as it continued to grow and evolve. The Walled Garden model of modern platforms made it difficult for users to remember what a filesystem was, leading to confusion about how to use the service. In 2014, the Gnutella project was abandoned by AOL, leading to its decline in popularity. Today, Gnutella remains an important part of the internet and continues to serve as a decentralized file-sharing solution for millions of users around the world.

articles.title: Flipper One — we need your help
articles.summary:
Flipper One is an open Linux platform that allows developers to build almost anything on it, from a 5G-enabled IP network analyzer to an SDR-powered radio signal analyzer with local AI. The project aims to reimagine what a Linux cyberdeck can be and push vendors to open up their existing closed-source code and ditch binary blobs entirely. Flipper One is an open platform that allows developers to build almost anything on it, from a 5G-enabled IP network analyzer to an SDR-powered radio signal analyzer with local AI.

The project has set itself ambitious goals: Build the most open and best-documented ARM computer in the world, with full mainline Linux kernel support. Push vendors to open up their existing closed-source code and ditch binary blobs entirely. Build an unconventional hardware platform based on a co-processor architecture that pairs a microcontroller with a CPU, and port tons of low-level MCU code.

Many of these goals come with a lot of uncertainty, which is scary.

articles.title: Aggressive AI scrapers are making it kinda suck to run wikis
articles.summary: Aggressive AI scrapers are making it harder for developers to run wikis, causing service outages and impacting wiki operations. These bots are using tens of millions of IP addresses to send requests that mimic human traffic, creating an incentive for them to craft the requests in a way that avoids detection by webmasters. This has led to the creation of new services like residential proxies, which can launder scraping requests through a large network of IP addresses. Developers need to be aware of these changes and take steps to mitigate their impact on wiki operations.
